section: Chapter 6: We Distribute, Yet Things Multiply

questions.question: What is the distributive property of multiplication over addition?
questions.explanation: Multiplication distributes over each term inside the bracket.

questions.question: What happens when a number multiplies a bracketed expression?
questions.correct_answer: It multiplies each term inside the bracket

questions.question: What is meant by expanding an expression?
questions.correct_answer: Removing brackets by applying the distributive property

questions.question: What is factorisation?
questions.correct_answer: Writing an expression as a product of its factors

questions.question: What is a common factor in algebra?
questions.correct_answer: A factor shared by all terms of an expression
questions.explanation: Example: In 8x + 16, the common factor is 8.

questions.question: What is meant by simplifying an algebraic expression?
questions.correct_answer: Reducing an expression to its simplest form
questions.explanation: This involves expanding brackets and combining like terms.

questions.question: What are like terms?
questions.correct_answer: Terms that have the same variable with the same power

questions.question: How do you combine like terms?
What is the reverse of expansion using distributive property?
Why is the distributive property important in algebra?
questions.correct_answer: It helps simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions
questions.explanation: It allows multiplication to be applied to each term inside brackets.

notes.title: Distributive Property
notes.content: Multiplication distributes over addition and subtraction: a(b + c) = ab + ac.

notes.title: Expansion
notes.content: Expansion means removing brackets using distributive property.

notes.title: Factorisation
notes.content: Factorisation is expressing an algebraic expression as a product of factors.

notes.title: Like Terms
notes.content: Like terms have the same variable and exponent and can be combined.

notes.title: Simplification
notes.content: Simplification involves expanding brackets and combining like terms.

facts.fact: Distributive property works for both addition and subtraction.
Factorisation is the reverse process of expansion.
Like terms can only be combined with other like terms.
Algebraic expressions can often be simplified using distributive property.
